comment.body:
Also, going back to Thursday, Alma said “spot up, vol up” and that higher vol means less liquidity.

However, when we rallied on Friday, volatility was down, yet volume was also significantly down compared to the 8 or so days prior.

So when interpreting whether the market has liquidity, isn’t volume far more correlative for liquidity than volatility, given this happened?

And if this is the case, then should volume be used to help to help build context as to when the proper liquidity will be in place to support the next leg lower?

comment.body:
Alma often refers to signs of lack of liquidity being related to large moves in the VIX. 

The idea is that when there are wide movements in the VIX that implies wide bid/ask spreads which is a good context for low liquidity. 

That’s my understanding
comment.raw_body:
